title: batemannia meleagris var. albidofulva
side_effects: Batemannia meleagris var. albidofulva can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions in sensitive individuals, which may manifest as skin irritation, itching, or swelling. Prolonged or excessive consumption of the plant's extract has been linked to stomach problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In rare cases, improper use of Batemannia meleagris var. albidofulva may also lead to interactions with other medications, potentially causing adverse effects such as dizziness, fatigue, or changes in blood pressure. Additionally, using the plant in uncontrolled doses or without proper medical supervision may exacerbate underlying health conditions, such as kidney or liver disease.
